aliases: Traditional Malaysian Coffee with Condensed Milk

english: Traditional Malaysian Coffee with Condensed Milk

food_subgroup: Hot Coffee Beverages — Traditional Kopitiam Style

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'coffee beverage' class (FOODON_03301737); no specific entry for Kopi Tarik exists. Closest parent match used; exact Kopi Tarik entry absent from FoodOn as of 2026.
ontology.foodex2_description: Coffee, with milk and sugar — closest FoodEx2 descriptor for a sweetened milky coffee beverage
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated Kopi Tarik entry. A00LC (coffee with milk and sugar) is the closest defensible descriptor. Documented as closest parent match.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Kopi Tarik or traditional pulled coffee beverages as of 2026.

preparation_method: pulling (repeated pouring between containers from height)
mixing with condensed milk and evaporated milk
main_ingredients: traditional kopi coffee beans (roasted with margarine or sugar)

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Kopi Tarik is approximately 250 ml (one cup), as commonly served at Malaysian and Singaporean kopitiams and mamak stalls.

nutrient_source.title: Energy and Nutrient Composition of Foods — HPB Singapore / IMR Malaysia MyFCD: Coffee with Condensed Milk (Kopi)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) and Institute for Medical Research Malaysia (IMR) MyFCD
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Kopi Tarik is a traditional pulled coffee with condensed and evaporated milk. Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore food composition data for 'Kopi' (coffee with condensed milk) and cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD entries for similar sweetened milky coffee beverages. The input values (energy 72 kcal, protein 1.5 g, fat 2.2 g, carbohydrate 11.5 g per 100 g) are consistent with HPB-published data for kopi-style beverages and are retained with medium confidence. Saturated fat estimated from condensed and evaporated milk fat profile per USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (condensed milk, evaporated milk entries).

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values — Atkinson et al. 2008; analogous estimate for sweetened milky coffee
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement for Kopi Tarik exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 55 is estimated by analogy to sweetened milky coffee beverages (coffee with condensed milk) documented in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of GI and GL Values, Diabetes Care 31(12):2281-2283. The fat and protein content of condensed and evaporated milk moderates the glycemic response relative to pure sugar solutions. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (55 × 28.75) / 100 = 15.8. Carbohydrate per 250 g serving = 11.5 × 2.5 = 28.75 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Caffeine content and moderate sugar load; sodium is relatively low at 45 mg per 100 g (112.5 mg per 250 ml serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit Kopi Tarik to one cup per day due to caffeine's transient blood pressure-raising effect, and should request reduced condensed milk ('siu dai') to lower sugar intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined sugar from condensed milk (10.8 g sugar per 100 g; approximately 27 g per 250 ml serving) causing significant postprandial glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid standard Kopi Tarik and instead choose 'Kopi O Kosong' (black coffee without sugar or milk) to eliminate the high glycemic load of approximately 15.8 per serving.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from condensed and evaporated milk (estimated 1.4 g per 100 g) and high sugar content contributing to dyslipidaemia risk with regular consumption
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit Kopi Tarik consumption and request 'siu dai' (less sweet) or switch to 'Kopi O' (without milk) to reduce saturated fat and sugar intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kopi Tarik is a traditional hot pulled coffee beverage iconic to Malaysian and Singaporean kopitiams (traditional coffeeshops) and mamak stalls. It is made from robusta-dominant 'kopi' beans that are roasted with margarine or sugar to produce a dark, rich, slightly caramelised flavour profile distinct from Western espresso-style coffee. The brewed coffee is combined with sweetened condensed milk and evaporated milk, then 'pulled' — poured repeatedly between two containers from a height — to create a thick, frothy head, smooth creamy texture, and uniform temperature. The pulling technique aerates the drink and blends the milk thoroughly. Per 100 ml, Kopi Tarik provides approximately 72 kcal, 1.5 g protein, 2.2 g fat, and 11.5 g carbohydrate (of which 10.8 g is sugar), making it a moderately energy-dense beverage when consumed in a standard 250 ml serving (approximately 180 kcal). The high sugar content from condensed milk makes it unsuitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes unless modified to 'Kopi O Kosong' (black, unsweetened). It is a staple breakfast and snack beverage across Malaysia and Singapore, deeply embedded in Malay, Chinese, and Indian Tamil cultural food traditions. Caffeine content warrants caution for individuals with hypertension or caffeine sensitivity.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Kopi Tarik?
Is Kopi Tarik suitable for diabetics?
What is the difference between Kopi Tarik and Teh Tarik?
Is Kopi Tarik high in sugar?
Can I drink Kopi Tarik if I have high blood pressure?
What milk is used in Kopi Tarik?
What is the glycemic index of Kopi Tarik?

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from HPB Singapore food composition data for kopi-style beverages and cross-referenced with IMR Malaysia MyFCD. No single approved source entry is an exact match for Kopi Tarik as a named dish; values are consistent with the known ingredient composition (condensed milk, evaporated milk, brewed robusta coffee) and are plausible. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for sweetened milky coffee. Confidence is medium rather than high due to the adapted rather than exact source match.
notes: 1. water_g set to -1: detailed moisture content for the final Kopi Tarik beverage as prepared (with pulling technique) is not separately reported in HPB Singapore or IMR Malaysia MyFCD databases as of 2026; the value is inherently high given the beverage nature but cannot be confirmed numerically from an approved source. This field is set to -1 per the Veritas numeric hygiene rule for data not available in the consulted approved sources (HPB Singapore food composition database and IMR Malaysia MyFCD). 2. Saturated fat (1.4 g/100 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.5 g/100 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1 g/100 g), and cholesterol_mg (8.0 mg/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entries for condensed milk (NDB 01097) and evaporated milk (NDB 01086) proportioned to the typical Kopi Tarik recipe ratio, as these values are not individually reported in the HPB or IMR source for this beverage. 3. trans_fat_g is reported as 0.0 (confirmed zero per USDA FoodData Central data for condensed and evaporated milk). 4. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0 (naturally zero — coffee beverage with no plant fibre-contributing solid ingredients). 5. GI of 55 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for Kopi Tarik exists in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026. 6. Ontology codes are closest parent matches; no exact FoodOn or FoodEx2 entry for Kopi Tarik exists as of 2026. 7. Multilingual names for Tamil and Hindi are transliterations of the Malay name as no distinct Tamil or Hindi name for this beverage is documented in approved sources; the drink is widely consumed by Tamil and Hindi-speaking communities in Malaysia and Singapore under the same Malay name.
